column4: Mathematical units inspired by biological neurons that compute weighted inputs and nonlinear activations.
Early trainable artificial neural networks using adjustable weights for binary pattern classification.
Rule-based AI systems that encode human expertise as symbols, inference rules, and knowledge bases.
Gradient-based algorithm for efficiently training multi-layer neural networks by propagating error derivatives backward.
Models such as Bayesian networks and Markov random fields that represent uncertain relationships with graph structure.
Supervised learning models that find maximum-margin decision boundaries, often using kernel functions for nonlinear classification.
Predictive methods such as random forests and gradient boosting that combine many decision trees for robust classification and regression.
Learning framework in which agents improve policies through rewards, actions, and interaction with environments.
Workflows for labeling data, extracting features, training models, validating performance, and deploying predictors.
Algorithms that find latent structure, clusters, embeddings, or density patterns in unlabeled data.
Tools and workflows for collecting, annotating, auditing, and versioning labeled datasets for machine learning.
Standardized datasets and leaderboards used to compare machine-learning systems and measure progress.
Neural networks using convolutional filters and hierarchical features for images, video, speech, and spatial data.
Vector representations of words learned from distributional patterns in large text corpora.
Neural architecture pattern that maps input sequences to output sequences while learning attention over relevant context.
Training approach that creates predictive learning signals from raw data without manually labeled targets.
Workflow of training large models on broad data and adapting them to downstream tasks with smaller curated datasets.
Large adaptable models trained on broad data that can be reused across many downstream tasks and modalities.
Post-training methods that tune language models to follow instructions and human preferences using demonstrations, rankings, and reward models.
Training method that aligns representations across modalities such as images and text using paired examples.
Systematic tests, benchmark suites, and evaluation harnesses for measuring model capabilities, robustness, fairness, and risks.
Operational tooling for model versioning, deployment, monitoring, rollback, feature pipelines, and scalable inference.
Design of natural-language instructions, examples, constraints, and tool schemas that steer foundation-model behavior at inference time.
Language models connected to APIs, code execution, search, retrieval, calculators, or external software tools.
Techniques for reducing harmful behavior, improving controllability, evaluating misuse risks, and aligning models with human intent.
AI systems that plan multi-step work, call tools, manage memory, monitor progress, and recover from errors with limited supervision.
AI systems that pair generated reasoning or plans with external checks, proofs, tests, simulations, or audits before action.
Compact personalized foundation models running locally on phones, laptops, vehicles, or robots with privacy-preserving adaptation.
